programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the new Secretary of the Navy, charges Gibbs and the team with finding terrorist group the Brotherhood of Doubt, which was responsible for killing her predecessor. She expects the group's next target to be billionaire Tomas Mendez, who spends his money trying to defeat terrorism, but whose motives may not be entirely altruistic. Meanwhile, Tony flies to Tel Aviv, where new Mossad director Orli Elbaz reveals they have a report of a possible sighting of Ziva at a kibbutz she went to as a child. Crime drama, guest starring Marina Sirtis.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. The owner of the vehicle turns out to be Vijay Chaya, the billionaire head of a large corporation, and the team suspects a case of mistaken identity. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. Guest starring Billy Dee Williams (The Empire Strikes Back) and Ralph Waite (The Waltons).(n)

programme.desc: The volunteers experience the living and working conditions of the 1870s, which was blighted by a dire economic depression. programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: A look at how Margaret Thatcher's struggling public image was transformed in 1982 when Argentine forces occupied the British overseas territory of the Falkland Islands in the South Atlantic, with the triumph of the British forces making her reputation in the UK and around the world. On the home front she faced another challenge to her leadership from National Union of Miners, which called a UK-wide strike, while in 1984 the IRA bombed the Grand Hotel in Brighten in an attempt to kill her and her most senior colleagues.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison compete to win a commission to transform a garden room in a three-bedroom Victorian terraced house in Shrewsbury for Mike and Lisa. Robert suggests a structure that gives each of them their own separate spaces, while Laura attempts to blend the needs of the whole family with in a highly adaptable garden room.(n)

programme.title: The Choir: Singing for Britain
programme.desc: Gareth Malone sets out to bring the nation together through music during the Coronavirus lockdown, by getting people from all walks of life to perform a specially composed song. The first episode focuses on frontline workers, all of whom have a courageous story to tell.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended edition of the show, in which an eclectic mix of five celebrities go out for dinner together. When they arrive at the restaurant, they must place their bank card in the centre of the table. As the food is served and the drinks flow, they play a series of fun and revealing games, and if they win a game, they can retrieve their card. The person whose card remains at the end of the evening must pick up the bill for the whole group. Taking part in the first edition are Rylan Clark-Neal, Ed Gamble, Carol Vorderman, Ellie Taylor and Anton Du Beke.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the six remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room to revive the glamorous, and sometimes ridiculous style, of the 1980s. Patrick Grant and Esme Young challenge the sewers to get down to business with the trickiest pattern so far, a woman's power jacket, before they are tasked with turning high-vis workman clothes into a party outfit in the Transformation Challenge. Finally in the Made to Measure Challenge, the contestants create their own versions of a cocktail dress.(n)

That 2-0 victory at Goodison Park back in November was their only away win of the season, and Farke will be hoping for nothing less than three points here in his side's attempt to avoid relegation.(n)

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: A look at how Margaret Thatcher and her government sought to transform the British economy with far-reaching consequences, while tensions surfaced amongst her closest allies as she tried to improve her public image and events revealed that the Iron Lady had emotional vulnerabilities in relation to her family.(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Summer Feasts
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain prepares an array of mouth-watering dishes that she will serve up to her loved ones at her own summer party feast, including a barbecued butterflied leg of lamb with sticky rhubarb glaze. She makes side dishes of kiwi and feta salad and edamame wild rice, as well as olive and rosemary bread shaped like a crown. For dessert, she goes all out with a magical princess torte cake and an ingenious raspberry ripple eclair, and creates a thirst-quenching drink in the form of an elderflower cooler.(n)

programme.title: On Chesil Beach (2017)
programme.desc: Edward and Florence, two young university graduates from very different backgrounds, decide to get married after an intense, but non-physical affair. When the pair embark on their honeymoon to Chesil Beach on the Dorset coast, they are both suddenly struck by their fear of the upcoming consummation of the marriage. Romantic drama, starring Saoirse Ronan and Billy Howle.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Stephen: The Murder That Changed a Nation
programme.desc: Final part of the documentary exploring Stephen Lawrence's murder and the subsequent police investigation. The public inquiry leads to a change in the law meaning the suspects can be retried. Detective Clive Driscoll takes on the case, and with the help of advances in forensic science he arrests Gary Dobson and David Norris. But as justice seems to have finally been done, revelations that the police spied on the Lawrence family begin to surface.(n)

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a fashion makeover show, with five stylists advising a diverse range of people in need of a significant style overhaul. In the first edition they help Chris, a 6ft 5in personal hygiene salesman who only wears black, and 5ft 3in Manny, who has been wearing children's clothes for years and is looking for a grown-up style with sass. PE teacher Rachel wants to swap gym wear for glamour, while warehouse employee Karen is worried that her work wardrobe is creeping into her weekend.(n)

programme.title: Jay Blades' Home Fix
programme.desc: Aided by a rich catalogue of BBC home and garden archive, Jay offers tips on ways to tackle outstanding tasks and encourages viewers to become self-sufficient in their own homes.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman presents a compilation of memorable episodes in Scotland, examining the country's diverse architectural styles and featuring a couple who relocated 800 miles.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The teams head to Newmarket Racecourse in Suffolk, where Roo Irvine and Tim Weeks help them search for valuables. Charlie Ross also visits the Scott Polar Research Institute.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality renovations for a three-bed home in Surrey, which has a poorly positioned front door and a cluttered hallway. The owners are divided over whether an extension would solve their problems, and the architects present them with two very different visions for what their home could be. Laura takes a characteristically pragmatic approach with a simple but striking contemporary design. But experimental architect Robert decides a far more radical approach is the only answer.(n)

programme.title: The Victorian Slum
programme.desc: The volunteers experience the living and working conditions of the 1890s, with cheap foodstuffs and mass-manufactured goods having found their way into the slum's shop - but only some of the residents can afford them. The Howarths' relative prosperity means Mandy can turn her attention to being a respectable Victorian house wife, while the Potters are offered a lifeline by their neighbour Maria, who needs help with her laundry business. Michael Mosley presents.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker, in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash.(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Denmark v Germany from Euro 92. A chance to see the third-place finisher in the public vote for the best European Championship final, which was held at Ullevi in Gothenburg, Sweden.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Caroline Hawley head to the National Maritime Museum in Falmouth, Cornwall, where they unearth items including an unusual tortoise and an electric guitar.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown presents a compilation of memorable properties featured on Escape to the Country, focusing on Shropshire, an increasingly popular destination for rural escapees.(n)

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: The concluding part of the documentary looks at how Margaret Thatcher's leadership style and the issues of Europe and the poll tax split her cabinet and created public anger, leading to her downfall. Protests against the poll tax resulted in violence in central London, but more damagingly there was widespread disenchantment in Tory strongholds across the country. Fractures emerge with senior cabinet colleagues, worsened by her European policy and Geoffrey Howe's resignation leads to a leadership challenge.(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix travels to Austria's capital Vienna in the company of food writer Rachel Khoo. The duo begin in the city's first district, the epicentre of coffee house culture, a crucial part of the Viennese social scene. Here, Rachel takes Fred to the iconic Café Demel, known for its exquisite cakes. Moving on, they learn how to make a spinach, cream and bread dumpling, before meeting a trailblazing chef taking the city by storm. Fred in particular is wowed by his schnitzel, reworked into a haute cuisine masterpiece. Finally the duo end their evening with a visit to one of the city's many sausage stands.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig talks garbage as she asks Alan Davies and this week's guest panellists, Bridget Christie, Johnny Vegas and Holly Walsh, to reveal all they know about rubbish.(n)

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: As Cesare bids the club a fond farewell, Mark surprises the squad by announcing coach Chris Booth as caretaker manager - infuriating Petey, who cannot believe he has been passed over for the job. Mattie is finally selected to start a match, Benji employs a social media manager to increase his online followers and Chris Booth takes on a sports psychologist in an attempt to boost team performance.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures - Chelsea v Manchester City at Stamford Bridge, Southampton v Arsenal at St Mary's Stadium and Burnley v Watford at Turf Moor. The Blues had lost their previous three encounters with City in all competitions, although last season's corresponding match ended in a 2-0 victory thanks to goals from N'Golo Kante and David Luiz.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Racism in the Ranks
programme.desc: Reporter Callum Tulley investigates allegations of bullying, harassment and discrimination in the British Army that come disproportionately from ethnic minority soldiers. He hears stories from ex-soldiers, some of whom were born in Commonwealth countries, and meets a soldier thrown out of the Army having shared racist material on an online group chat.(n)

programme.title: Books That Made Britain
programme.desc: Martha Kearney investigates why East Anglia has inspired so many great crime writers, including PD James and Nicci French.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a landscape with a glowing evening sky on top of a black canvas.(n)

programme.title: Hidden Killers
programme.desc: In the first five years of King Edward's reign there were 140,000 British patents issued, many using electricity. However, as historian Suzannah Lipscomb discovers, there was no regulation of this new energy, and cables were not insulated or appliances earthed to today's exacting standards. As a response to electrocutions and fires, asbestos was employed, but it would be some time until its own risks to health would be fully appreciated. Suzannah also looks at other dubious products associated with the era, such as the radioactive clock and arsenic soap.(n)

programme.title: Ancient Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Michael Scott uses 3D scanning technology to reveal the historical secrets of ancient Athens and tell the story of how this remarkable city created the world's first democracy. He begins his journey on the Acropolis, where, in the late sixth century BC the people of Athens overthrew a tyrant and set up a democracy, and investigates a mysterious, asymmetrical temple called the Erechtheion that sits in the shadow of the world-famous Parthenon. To the south of the city, he clambers through a silver mine where an army of slaves once toiled inside the labyrinth of narrow tunnels.(n)

programme.title: Against the Law
programme.desc: Fact-based drama based on the 1954 Montagu Trial, in which gay journalist Peter Wildeblood and his friends Lord Montagu and Michael Pitt-Rivers were tried for homosexual offences. The ensuing scandal and public outcry over the fairness of the case forced a reluctant government to set up a committee to investigate whether homosexuality should be legalised. Starring Daniel Mays and Mark Gatiss and featuring testimony from gay men who lived through those days, a retired police officer and a former psychiatric nurse.(n)

programme.title: Prejudice and Pride: The People's History of LGBTQ Britain
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary in which people share the items that have defined their lives in the 50 years since homosexuality was partially decriminalised in 1967. They include a rare collection of the first openly gay magazine - featuring the rising young singer David Bowie - to letters from worried parents of newly 'out' daughters and sons. Presented by Stephen K Amos and Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: Brighton: 50 Years of Gay
programme.desc: Simon Fanshawe marks the 50th anniversary of the Sexual Offences Act 1967, which which decriminalised private homosexual acts between men aged over 21. The writer and broadcaster examines this landmark change in the law and reveals the story of the fight for gay equality through the colourful history of his home town of Brighton.(n)

programme.title: Cleopatra and Me: In Search of a Lost Queen
programme.desc: Shakespeare scholar Islam Issa grew up in Birmingham with his Egyptian-born parents. As a child he was taught Cleopatra was a great politician, who turned her capitol Alexandria into a global seat of learning and made Egypt one of the most powerful countries on the planet. But as a teenager, he realised she had a very different reputation in Britain - either a tragic Shakespearean heroine or a seductress, portrayed by screen sirens like Elizabeth Taylor. In this programme, he sets out to discover the story behind these two Cleopatras.(n)

programme.title: Books That Made Britain
programme.desc: Martha Kearney investigates why East Anglia has inspired so many great crime writers, including PD James and Nicci French.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a landscape with a glowing evening sky on top of a black canvas.(n)

To the south of the city, he clambers through a silver mine where an army of slaves once toiled inside the labyrinth of narrow tunnels.(n)

programme.title: Books That Made Britain
programme.desc: Gemma Cairney is joined by authors Philippa Gregory, Mike Manson and Melvin Burgess, who reveal how the dark side of Bristol inspired their fiction writing.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a little wooden shanty upon a bed of soft snow in the middle of a cold, wintry forest.(n)

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Historian Bettany Hughes investigates the ideas of ancient philosophers, starting with the Indian nobleman Siddhartha Gautama, more popularly known as Buddha. Thought to have been lived and tought between the sixth and fourth centuries BC, the sage and holy man inspired a diverse belief system that influences the lives of millions of people to this day. She travels to India, where the Buddha experienced the challenging ideas and extreme methods of wandering 'truth seekers', after he had abandoned his family and homeland in the Himalayas to embark on his philosophical quest to find a solution to human suffering.(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary examining how the National Enquirer became the most infamous tabloid in America by churning out obscene stories that blur the lines between truth and fiction.(n)

programme.title: The Making of Spain
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore examines Spain's Golden Age under Philip II through to the Spanish Civil War and dictatorship under Franco, from which Spain has emerged as a modern democratic monarchy.(n)

programme.title: Prejudice and Pride: The People's History of LGBTQ Britain
programme.desc: The second of two documentaries in which people share the items that have defined their lives in the 50 years since homosexuality was partially decriminalised in 1967. Focusing on the past 30 years, it features the nun-impersonating freedom-fighting Sisters of Perpetual Indulgence, the writer behind a famous lesbian kiss and a Muslim man who set up an LGBTQ support group for South East Asians. Presented by Susan Calman and Stephen K Amos.(n)

programme.title: Books That Made Britain
programme.desc: Gemma Cairney is joined by authors Philippa Gregory, Mike Manson and Melvin Burgess, who reveal how the dark side of Bristol inspired their fiction writing.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Rejected by Elizabeth, Darcy decides to return to Rosling Park - and resolves to reveal the truth about Wickham's character in a letter. Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha star.(n)

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World

programme.title: Books That Made Britain
programme.desc: Chris Packham takes a tour of the Cornish coast and reveals the stories behind five classic books set in the county, including Winston Graham's Poldark and Daphe du Maurier's Frenchman's Creek.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Another half hour in the company of American artist Bob Ross as he puts paint to canvas. Today he takes viewers on a summertime nature walk ending at a secluded lake.(n)

programme.title: Tess of the D'Urbervilles
programme.desc: Tess's baby Sorrow dies after falling seriously ill and she is forced to bury him alone when Parson Tringham refuses. She then leaves home and finds work at a dairy farm, where she falls in love with Angel Clare and agrees to marry him. However, the bride-to-be suffers an attack of conscience the night before her wedding and decides to tell her prospective husband about her past. Gemma Arterton stars.(n)

programme.title: Boy George's 1970s: Save Me from Suburbia
programme.desc: The BBC's My Generation season continues as Boy George revisits his teenage years. While history may remember the 1970s as being marked by strikes, poverty and civil discord, the singer reveals that - for the young George O'Dowd - the decade was the beginning of his musical, social and sexual coming-of-age. He discusses how sexual repression and disenfranchisement, along with the advent of glam rock, disco and punk, gave rise to the flamboyant persona he would later unleash on the world, via Culture Club's memorable first appearance on Top of the Pops in 1982.(n)

programme.title: Glastonbury (2006)
programme.desc: Julien Temple's documentary about the world-famous music festival, which began in 1970 and continues to attract a diverse and enthusiastic range of revellers. The film features performances from artists including Morrissey, David Bowie, Bjork, James Brown, Nick Cave, Joe Strummer, Billy Bragg and Blur, while amateur footage provides an authentic taste of the event.(n)

programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Crystal Palace at Anfield and Manchester United v Sheffield United at Old Trafford. The Reds held an excellent recent record over the Eagles, having won their last five top-flight encounters - a run stretching back to April 2017. History was also on the Red Devils' side, with their last league defeat against the Blades coming back in 1992. Plus, action from Newcastle United v Aston Villa at St James' Park, Norwich City v Everton at Carrow Road and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Bournemouth at Molineux.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contest, hosted by Joe Lycett, reaches its final stage as the last three contestants take on a trio of celebration wear challenges before the winner is announced. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young start by challenging the sewers to make a little boys kilt without a pattern. In the Transformation Challenge, they each attempt to turn party dresses into fun outfits fit to wear at a carnival, before finally creating unique gowns using luxury fabric in the Made to Measure challenge. At the end of the three challenges, one of the competitors is crowned this year's winner.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Crystal Palace at Anfield and Manchester United v Sheffield United at Old Trafford. The Reds held an excellent recent record over the Eagles, having won their last five top-flight encounters - a run stretching back to April 2017. History was also on the Red Devils' side, with their last league defeat against the Blades coming back in 1992. Plus, action from Newcastle United v Aston Villa at St James' Park, Norwich City v Everton at Carrow Road and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Bournemouth at Molineux.(n)

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Comedy in which actors David Tennant and Michael Sheen play themselves as they prepare for a stage production while under lockdown. In the penultimate episode, Simon brings in a friend to try and convince David and Michael to put their faith in him. Meanwhile, Michael spies an ambulance next door and worries about a neighbour, and David manages to keep afloat in Georgia's absence. Not only keeping the house in order and the home-schooling ticking over, but David also gets around to finishing a draft of his screenplay.(n)

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: In the final episode, the play's producer decides to draft in a voice of experience to get Michael and David in line. Meanwhile, Georgia shares David's work without him knowing, and he is pleasantly surprised by the enthusiastic response his work receives, and Michael seeks resolution on what has happened to his neighbour. Comedy, starring Michael Sheen and David Tennant.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A missing woman staggers into the police station, claiming a creature is 'trying to get out'. That night her father panics as he sees an alien shape through the window. Mark Williams stars.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is on a mission to help find a country home for £300,000 in the East Midlands for an eager buyer who has brought along her friendly neighbour for support. programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: Harriet Walter stars in this updated version of Alan Bennett's dramatic monologue Soldiering On, which originally starred Stephanie Cole. After the death of her husband, Muriel is left looking after her mentally ill daughter. She's a strong woman and always has been. But when her son Giles turns up with papers for her to sign, it turns out she is quite a rich widow. But can he be trusted?(n)

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the fashion makeover show, with volunteers including an Essex pawnbroker who has put off his wedding three times because he has lost confidence in his looks and a biker chick from Newport Pagnell, who rocks up wanting a funked-up feminine look. The experts also get their hands on a stay-at-home mum who wants her fashion mojo back after battling a brain condition and a lifelong military man who would like a smart casual look perfect for a single gentleman in his sixties. Plus, advice for viewers on what jeans work best for different body shapes.(n)

programme.title: Make Me Famous
programme.desc: One-off drama about the impact of fame on reality TV stars. One year ago, Billy thought his life was set to change for ever after appearing on a constructed reality show. But while some of his co-stars' careers are thriving, a series of tabloid kiss-and-tells force Billy's vulnerabilities to the surface. Can he escape the reputation that now precedes him? Starring Tom Brittney, Amanda Abbington and Aiysha Hart. Written by presenter and DJ Reggie Yates.(n)

programme.desc: Daniel De Simone investigates a global network of neo-Nazis that aims to destroy society, discovering that it is recruiting in the UK. Police say right-wing extremism is the fastest growing terrorist threat in the UK and that the coronavirus pandemic may be leaving young people vulnerable to radicalisation. As Daniel investigates he notices certain names cropping up again and again and tracks down some of the movement's most influential figures and reveals how the network operates across the globe.(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his dealers are in Buxton, Derbyshire. A collection of cartoon maps brought to Henry Nicholls' table surprises everybody at the auction, and Tim Hogarth buys a mint condition Winnie the Pooh first edition book. Plus, dealer Jan Keyne can't stop dancing when she hears the sound of a 1950s radio.(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A team of British Army colleagues attempts to topple the Tenable Tower of 10 to win a big cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis.(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Ben, Clive, Danielle and Hope pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The curmudgeonly GP is perturbed when he discovers Louisa, who is already spending much of her time away from home, has applied to take up a child and adolescent counselling course. However, achieving a work-life balance is far from the greatest of concerns for some of the Ellinghams' neighbours, not least of whom is Al, whose plans to demonstrate his business acumen are placed in jeopardy when Ken decides to close the pub. Unless Ruth agrees to serve as a guarantor, Ken will not allow Morwenna and Al to take over the establishment, so the pair arrange a race-walking event to raise funds and prove the community is on their side.(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts six more contestants through their paces to see who will have the opportunity to answer 15 questions to win the prize of £1,000,000. Each player has four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal.(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. On the third and final leg of his odyssey, he begins in Northern Canada before travelling into the icy wilderness of Alaska. Along the way, Alexander meets people who have headed north to discover adventure and seek their fortune, as well as digging for gold with modern day prospectors. He also takes on the challenge of driving up one of the deadliest roads in the world, before concluding his travels on the international dateline between America and Russia.(n)

programme.desc: The actor and documentary-maker continues his examination into the impact of dementia, meeting a family whose five-year-old girl has a form of the disease and a woman who needs to sell her mother's house to pay for her £70,000 annual care home fees. Ross also talks once more to Scott Mitchell, husband of his former colleague Barbara Windsor, to find out how they have been coping during lockdown.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp Living With
programme.desc: An investigation into the problem of addiction to opioids - opium-derived drugs - which are used in powerful prescription painkillers. Ross meets a woman who ended up with a serious addiction after being prescribed drugs for her bad back. He also learns about fentanyl, a painkiller used in cancer treatment, which can be much stronger than heroin and is being advertised for sale illegally in the UK. Ross comes face to face with a drug dealer who has started cutting fentanyl into his product and meets a former chemist whose daughter died from an overdose after taking heroin laced with the drug.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: Two returning families compete in the first semi-final of the knockout tournament, bringing them one step closer to the chance to win £25,000 for their chosen charity and the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family. The Chase's Anne Hegerty, aka The Governess, asks the questions.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The curmudgeonly GP is perturbed when he discovers Louisa, who is already spending much of her time away from home, has applied to take up a child and adolescent counselling course. However, achieving a work-life balance is far from the greatest of concerns for some of the Ellinghams' neighbours, not least of whom is Al, whose plans to demonstrate his business acumen are placed in jeopardy when Ken decides to close the pub. Unless Ruth agrees to serve as a guarantor, Ken will not allow Morwenna and Al to take over the establishment, so the pair arrange a race-walking event to raise funds and prove the community is on their side.(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts six more contestants through their paces to see who will have the opportunity to answer 15 questions to win the prize of £1,000,000. Each player has four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal.(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. On the third and final leg of his odyssey, he begins in Northern Canada before travelling into the icy wilderness of Alaska. Along the way, Alexander meets people who have headed north to discover adventure and seek their fortune, as well as digging for gold with modern day prospectors. He also takes on the challenge of driving up one of the deadliest roads in the world, before concluding his travels on the international dateline between America and Russia.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan becomes jealous when he discovers Francine was once engaged to a pilot whose plane crashed. However, it emerges his wife's would-be husband survived the incident, and the envious spy enlists Roger's help to track him down.(n)

programme.desc: Cleveland Jr and Kendra petition for a bill granting equal rights to overweight people, but the move is rejected and they flee to the more tolerant surroundings of Wisconsin, where they feel accepted.(n)

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling is joined by new team captain Scarlett Moffatt as three friends take on celebrities Jack Fincham, Natasia Demetriou, Yasmin Evans and Richard Arnold.(n)

programme.title: Release the Hounds
programme.desc: Joey Essex, Charlotte Crosby and Pete Bennett take part in a celebrity version of the horror game show, where they enter a forest at dusk and endure a series of spooky challenges during which they must unlock chests full of prize money. In order to win the jackpot they need to get back out again, but the cash is protected by a pack of angry dogs. Hosted by Reggie Yates.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: The host talks to Savannah Guthrie and Hoda Kotb about Today, and James Bay performs Ain't No Sunshine in a tribute to Grammy-winning soul singer Bill Withers.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lockdown is a trial for Marlon who is living in another house due to plumbing issues while his kids are residing in other properties. Matters are made worse when he discovers Ellis has invited his father Al to live with them as he has nowhere else to go.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David is amused when Shona steals Ray's wallet and explains that is how they met, while Daniel opens up to Nicky and tells her about Sinead. Leanne's thrilled to see a flash of the old Oliver at a petting zoo, and Peter is offended when Carla accuses him of beating up Jordan and Chelsey.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, including footage of a golfer being surprised by an alligator, a child being fired into a belfry, and a restaurant receiving an unexpected guest through their ceiling.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy discovers Mateo and Jeff are dating, Dina suspects Amy faked an illness, and Glenn and Jonah comes to blows when Glenn realises Jonah is outshining him.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Jonah and Amy travel to her childhood home to help her parents move out. Glenn and Garrett vie for the Cloud 9 Integrity Award, while Dina uses Mateo's relationship with Jeff to fix problems within the store. America Ferrera stars.(n)

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Grace from Kingston upon Thames picks three blind dates from five potential partners, based entirely on the menus they have created. When the three dinners have been eaten, she decides who she wants to see again. Narrated by Charlotte Hudson.(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Londoner Frankie puts her faith in the fashion choices of three single men - but only one will win a date with her.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill introduces another rib-tickling collection of camcorder calamities and home-video howlers, featuring practical jokers, naughty animals, musical mishaps and holiday clips.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a builder dropping in through a roof, a boy getting his head trapped in a mailbox, a woman being attacked by a remote-controlled plane and a man hit by a giant trampoline during a hurricane.(n)

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Ricky Martin joins Ellen for a chat and performs a song from his upcoming album. Plus, CNN news anchor Jake Tapper talks about covering the pandemic.(n)

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: The matchmaking series sees Robbie from Hampshire dine with three lucky women. When the dinners have been eaten and the dates are done, who might he want to see for seconds?(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Single women from Hertfordshire, Kent and Essex compete for the affections of musician Jack. Whose fashion choices will help them win a date?(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: The latest group of men entering the 'love lift' are a tech-savvy personal trainer, an airline pilot, a magician and musician, and a city banker, all hoping to impress a studio full of single women and win a date - but must first get the 30 female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Paddy McGuinness presents.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill judges the funniest 100 talent videos - karaoke disasters, battle stunts going wrong and stage falls. Magicians, acrobats, jugglers, dancers, singers and musicians all battle it out for the un-coveted Number one slot.(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers go wild in the aisles to win a £3,000 prize.(n)

programme.title: Roswell, New Mexico
programme.desc: A power cut across town leaves patients in the hospital in need of help. Liz learns more details about the night Rosa died. Sci-fi drama, starring Jeanine Mason.(n)

programme.title: Love Island: Australia
programme.desc: New arrival Mac goes speed dating, while Elias and Millie go from hot to not after a night in the Hideaway.(n)

programme.title: Killer Camp
programme.desc: The campers face more fiendish camp activities, including blindly putting their hands inside terrifying boxes for cash, and smacking each other with soaking dodgeballs.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz tells Des she knows Sean wants to get rid of her. Curly goes into work and breaks the news of his marriage. Rita and Mavis return from their cruise.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim is amazed when Liz tells him she has been laid off by Des. The Firmans entertain Curly and Raquel. Vicky buys Steve a personalised number plate for Christmas.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is devastated after her disastrous date with Dave. Robert insists the Dingles stole the cow but Jack is unconvinced. Scott is still being menaced by Marcus.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles do not know where the cow has gone. Eric plans to host a singles night at the wine bar. Linda confides in Zoe about her problems.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Paying guests the Langleys arrive for a shooting party at Ashfordly Hall, but their grandiose ideas upset the locals and it is not long before a sinister plot is revealed. Vernon's latest money-making scheme seems destined to end in disaster as David takes the wheel of an old Army vehicle, but his off-road tours are not for the faint-hearted. Nostalgic rural drama, guest starring Robert Glenister and Celia Imrie.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The age gap between Sid and his children becomes apparent when he returns home early from work. Comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid arranges a fishing weekend, blissfully unaware Jean's father is coming to stay. Classic family comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: When the trio decide they need a fourth person to share the flat, Robin is determined it will be a woman. Seventies comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A father is aghast to read tales of wild romance in his teenage daughter's diary, failing to realise they are all fictitious. David inherits a ventriloquist's dummy complete with a schedule of bookings, and decides the show must go on. Meanwhile, Gina finds herself attracted to a charismatic guest. Nostalgic drama, starring David Lonsdale and Tricia Penrose.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda and Biff start the day with an argument. Eric encourages Seth to come to the singles night. Scott is shocked when Marcus shows him he has a gun.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel is upset that she let Curly down. Steve enjoys giving out bottles of whisky as Christmas presents. Jim cannot understand why Liz will not let him anywhere near Des.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera has an exhausting Christmas running the Rovers. Curly and Raquel have some explaining to do. Vicky despairs of Steve.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A horse whisperer disappears in Newton Magna, bringing Barnaby face to face with long-standing adversary Melvyn Stockard. The former convict's social climbing has paid dividends, sending him straight to the top of the village pecking order - and some believe his driving ambition makes him capable of foul play. Murder mystery, guest starring Larry Lamb (EastEnders, Gavin & Stacey), with John Nettles and Daniel Casey.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A father is aghast to read tales of wild romance in his teenage daughter's diary, failing to realise they are all fictitious. David inherits a ventriloquist's dummy complete with a schedule of bookings, and decides the show must go on. Meanwhile, Gina finds herself attracted to a charismatic guest. Nostalgic drama, starring David Lonsdale and Tricia Penrose.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mary begins to emerge from her grief, and takes an interest in the running of the estate. However, her ambitions meet firm resistance from her father. Lady Rose MacClare, Lady Flintshire's daughter, finds herself in a predicament at a tea dance in York, while Mrs Hughes urges Carson to heal an old wound. Bates comes up with a plan to help Molesley, but will it work? Costume drama, starring Michelle Dockery, Hugh Bonneville, Lily James, Phyllis Logan, Jim Carter, Brendan Coyle and Kevin Doyle.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Coma victim Harriet Farmer finally wakes up a year after she was attacked while trying to elope, but her boyfriend Finn Robson is still missing. Soon after she regains consciousness, members of a chess club are targeted by a killer. Could the assault that left Harriet for dead be linked to moves on a games board? Guest starring Fay Ripley (Cold Feet), with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack tackles what promises to be the most sensitive and unenviable investigation of his entire career - deciding whether a man with Down's syndrome is capable of committing murder. David Jason stars as the caustic copper, with Bruce Alexander and Matthew Marsh.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert is pleased to have Andy staying. Eric's tables have been ruined by the table top dancers. Terry comforts a drunken Viv outside the Woolpack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is surprised to see Steve back in the village. Viv does her best to avoid Terry. Betty is not happy when she finds out Seth attended the singles night.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve spends another night in the cells. Mavis refuses to go to Angela and Norris's wedding. Vicky calls in Uncle Nick to help Steve, assuring him that Steve is innocent.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mavis urges Derek not to upset Norris as he is his boss. Liz is upset that her son has turned out to be a liar and a thief. Des tells Raquel she was stupid to marry Curly.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prominent resident is robbed and battered to death with her walking stick, and suspicion soon falls on the members of her reading club - which turns out to be a front for a lucrative investment portfolio. John Nettles and Daniel Casey star, with guest appearances by Anton Rodgers (Fresh Fields) and Angela Thorne (To the Manor Born).(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina is suspected of being a hit-and-run driver, while Vernon is hired to escort two singers to a gig - but soon discovers he has an aversion to loud music and offloads the responsibility onto David, who is no match for the conniving pair.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: An extravagant party at the Abbey gives the Crawleys an opportunity to reunite with old friends. Some guests, however, prove more welcome than others, and alongside the celebrations come skulduggery and heartache. Includes guest appearances by Former EastEnders heart-throb Nigel Harman (Dennis Rickman) and soprano Kiri Te Kanawa, who plays the role of real-life singer Dame Nellie Melba.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A student production of The Merchant of Venice is halted when the actor playing Shylock is murdered during the performance. Lewis and Hathaway are struck by the lack of grief among the cast and crew - especially the ambitious director, who wastes no time recasting the role. The detectives discover the victim had an expensive lifestyle and not only owed money to his fellow students, but was also suspected of stealing from them. However, the investigation has barely got under way before the killer strikes again. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: It's 1946 and although one war has ended, another is looming as the Iron Curtain falls across Europe. Foyle arrives home from America, where he is wanted in connection with a suspicious suicide, but MI5 offers him a way out. The agency suspects Britain's atomic research programme has been infiltrated and wants to recruit him to investigate a Soviet spy ring that could be at work in the heart of London. Detective drama, starring Michael Kitchen, with Honeysuckle Weeks, Ellie Haddington and Dylan Charles.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Gruff detective Jack Frost's plans for a quiet weekend are disrupted by the gruesome discovery of an unidentified body floating in the river. However, soon after he has begun the investigation, he is forced to apply his deductive skills to a separate case involving an act of violence during a football match which left a player in a coma. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Larry Lamb, Bruce Alexander, Lindy Whiteford and Berwick Kaler.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean is delighted when Sid actually agrees with her for once, but their plans for the day end in disaster. Comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.desc: The flatmates crave a bit of summer warmth, and Mrs Roper's sunlamp mysteriously disappears. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.desc: Tricia is over the moon as she and Mike finally declare their love for one another - but gets drawn into a case of domestic violence before she has chance to celebrate, and ends up fighting for her life. Starring Clare Calbraith and Jason Durr.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda is nervous before her driving test. Nurse Bell orders Chris to leave Kim alone. Biff has a big surprise in store for Linda.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe nags Susie about losing her job. Linda and Biff are happy about getting officially engaged. Zak unveils his latest money-making scheme.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Mallets have a cold start to the year. Audrey gets emotional at the thought of a video existing of Alf and Betty with the Queen.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Judy worries about Gary after he suffers an electric shock. Denise is unhappy when Brian calls on her unexpectedly. Curly gets fed up with Raquel working every evening.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a childless farmer's wife puts Barnaby on the scent of an ancient land feud over Setwale Wood. Potions, witchcraft, marital infidelity and a ruthless campaign to save the area form the heady brew that convinces the detective he is dealing with a case of murder. Wendy Craig guest stars, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Tricia is over the moon as she and Mike finally declare their love for one another - but gets drawn into a case of domestic violence before she has chance to celebrate, and ends up fighting for her life. Starring Clare Calbraith and Jason Durr.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the Abbey's eventful party, Mary is forced to answer a pressing question about her future, while Carson reveals surprisingly intimate details about his former life. Rose's trip to the bright lights of London sees her risking the Crawleys' reputation, and Gregson shares ambitious plans with Edith, taking their relationship to another level. Costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When dairy worker Debbie Moffett (played by Martine McCutcheon) is crushed to death by a giant round of cheese at the home of the world-famous Midsomer Blue, Barnaby and Jones are soon on the case. Pathologist Kate Wilding discovers the victim's daughter was being expelled from the local prep school and it emerges Debbie died just hours after an argument at a parents' council meeting. Her phone leads the detectives to Oliver Ordish, with whom she was having an affair.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's investigation into the death of a nameless Russian leads to a mysterious military facility run by Colonel Harry Galt, a respected soldier with an impeccable war record. However, as Foyle makes inquiries, he realises the deceased was a spy with dangerous connections to British Intelligence. Meanwhile, Sam does her best to help husband Adam during his by-election campaign, but can't help putting her foot in it. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks, with Jonathan Hyde and Daniel Weyman.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A student production of The Merchant of Venice is halted when the actor playing Shylock is murdered during the performance. Lewis and Hathaway are struck by the lack of grief among the cast and crew - especially the ambitious director, who wastes no time recasting the role. The detectives discover the victim had an expensive lifestyle and not only owed money to his fellow students, but was also suspected of stealing from them. However, the investigation has barely got under way before the killer strikes again. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: It's 1946 and although one war has ended, another is looming as the Iron Curtain falls across Europe. Foyle arrives home from America, where he is wanted in connection with a suspicious suicide, but MI5 offers him a way out. The agency suspects Britain's atomic research programme has been infiltrated and wants to recruit him to investigate a Soviet spy ring that could be at work in the heart of London. Detective drama, starring Michael Kitchen, with Honeysuckle Weeks, Ellie Haddington and Dylan Charles.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Gruff detective Jack Frost's plans for a quiet weekend are disrupted by the gruesome discovery of an unidentified body floating in the river. However, soon after he has begun the investigation, he is forced to apply his deductive skills to a separate case involving an act of violence during a football match which left a player in a coma. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Larry Lamb, Bruce Alexander, Lindy Whiteford and Berwick Kaler.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a childless farmer's wife puts Barnaby on the scent of an ancient land feud over Setwale Wood. Potions, witchcraft, marital infidelity and a ruthless campaign to save the area form the heady brew that convinces the detective he is dealing with a case of murder. programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the Abbey's eventful party, Mary is forced to answer a pressing question about her future, while Carson reveals surprisingly intimate details about his former life. Costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When dairy worker Debbie Moffett (played by Martine McCutcheon) is crushed to death by a giant round of cheese at the home of the world-famous Midsomer Blue, Barnaby and Jones are soon on the case. Pathologist Kate Wilding discovers the victim's daughter was being expelled from the local prep school and it emerges Debbie died just hours after an argument at a parents' council meeting. Her phone leads the detectives to Oliver Ordish, with whom she was having an affair.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's investigation into the death of a nameless Russian leads to a mysterious military facility run by Colonel Harry Galt, a respected soldier with an impeccable war record. However, as Foyle makes inquiries, he realises the deceased was a spy with dangerous connections to British Intelligence. Meanwhile, Sam does her best to help husband Adam during his by-election campaign, but can't help putting her foot in it. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks, with Jonathan Hyde and Daniel Weyman.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the turn of exuberant hair salon owner Chris to host the final dinner party in Gloucestershire with home-made treats on the menu, including a tiger prawn starter, venison for the main and a dessert of chocolate and walnut ice-cream. Chat around the table reveals some differing opinions after the guests make a discovery in the host's living room, and the group reflect on their time together before the winner of the cash prize is announced.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A Yorkshire can-can dancer in France teaches her amateur troupe a classic routine, while in Spain, a Buckinghamshire couple's cycling holiday venture hits the skids.(n)

programme.title: Countdown

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Having suffered a close bereavement and realising life is increasingly short, Jo Tweddle and her husband Rich have decided to seize the day and move to somewhere new together. Rich wants to head to north Wales, an area which he loves, while Jo would prefer to move to the hills around the beautiful city of Granada in southern Spain. Jonnie Irwin pulls out all the stops to keep them in beautiful Snowdonia with a budget of £300,000, while Laura Hamilton does everything she can to persuade Rich to buy in Granada with three stunning properties for up to £400,000.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Newlyweds with a love of dancing hunt for a home in Almeria, while in Alicante, a Liverpudlian couple want a house with plenty of outside space for the husband's potted plants.(n)

programme.desc: The final visit is to the Beach House in Portland, Dorset, where Pete Lacey feels his back-to-basics approach offers his guests good value. But things get heated with the radiator in Karen and Liam's room and opinions are laid bare over dinner.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Britain's Best Parent?
programme.desc: Over the past four weeks, 12 couples with widely different parenting styles have competed to prove their way is the best. Now the winning four return to face the ultimate challenge - looking after each other's children overnight and away from home - before returning to the studio to discuss how it went. It's then up to the studio audience to choose which they believe to be the best parenting style - and who deserves to be crowned Britain's Best Parent. Presented by Anita Rani.(n)

programme.title: The School That Tried to End Racism
programme.desc: Documentary about a pioneering British school helping 11 and 12-year-old pupils uncover and eradicate hidden racial biases by giving them a series of activities designed to challenge everything they thought they knew about race. To begin with the students are split into different work groups according to their skin colour. A white British student and his black Caribbean best friend are shocked to discover the level of unconscious bias they carry, while a 12-year-old bi-racial girl is keen to find out what group she belongs in - white or black?(n)

programme.title: Educating Yorkshire

programme.desc: Graeme Hall continues to find solutions for dogs with problems. After a break-up, Hampshire woman Rebecca leaned on her Manchester terrier Marney for support. But now Marney will not let anyone new into her owner's life. In London, Jasper and Alexis cannot live a normal life because their Irish terrier Albert guards the front door like a bouncer.(n)

programme.title: This Week on the Farm
programme.desc: At Cannon Hall farm, Rob and Dave prepare their bull for mating and relive their childhood by camping out under the stars. A vet pays a visit to a rescue farm near Dewsbury to attend to a boar that keeps trying to escape, while the owners of the real Downton Abbey wait to see if they can expect a new generation of piglets. Plus, a look at how farming is changing under lockdown, including a vegetable supplier's innovative search for new customers.(n)

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben Fogle is reunited with seafarer Rick, who has moved on to pastures new since their original encounter. When Rick last appeared on the series, he was living off the coast of French Polynesia, but he has since made a fresh start on the Pacific island of Fiji. In the intervening years, Rick has acquired a new boat and is now travelling alone - but remains as enthusiastic as ever about his unusual lifestyle and believes that anyone can follow in his wake and gain a newfound sense of freedom - if they have the right attitude.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant travels across Europe to examine the train's role in the First World War. In Northumberland, he enjoys a ride on a preserved locomotive to find out how important the railways were to Britain for mobilisation of troops and the operation of large training camps around the country. Aboard a holiday train commandeered to transport troops and ammunition, Chris tells the stories of two opposing soldiers, one English and one German, who both took trains to the Somme in 1916.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Nazi Mega Bunker
programme.desc: A look at the extraordinary defences and kill zones that surrounded Hitler's bunker in Berlin, a subterranean safehouse protected by 13-foot-thick walls.(n)

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed.(n)

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: A Lilliputian lope round the Inner Hebrides puts Jane in touch with her Scottish roots, as alongside just six other passengers the singer embarks on what is easily her smallest ever cruise. Jane enjoys unseasonably cloudless skies and sparkling seas as the route takes in the puffin-packed Treshnish Islands and Fingal's Cave on Staffa, the mythic rock formation that famously inspired Mendelssohn's Hebrides Overture. The excursion is rounded off with some traditional trappings, from ceilidhs and weaving to haggis-making.(n)

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice tackles a period property in Bow, in the heart of London's East End, hoping to attract potential buyers by clearing clutter and creating an elegant dining area. Among other improvements, she ditches a navy blue bathroom suite in favour of one in stylish white to ensure the house will fetch the asking price of £300,000.(n)

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Historian Tim Marlow explores the life and work of 17th-century visionary Dutch artist Rembrandt Harmenszoon van Rijn, who was famed for his skilful handling of light and shade in paintings portraying the human form. Born in Leiden in 1606, the eighth of nine children, his passion for art led him to an apprenticeship with a local painter, and from the time he was a 19-year-old he had a studio in Amsterdam, where he produced 75 self-portraits. Demand for his talents soon grew and success came when he was commissioned to paint The Anatomy Lesson of Dr Tulp.(n)

programme.title: Are They a Con?
programme.desc: Paul Connolly and Jean Johansson present a consumer show investigating claims made by shops and advertisers. In the first edition, they examine pre-prepared food, meeting a Birmingham family whose two ready-meal mad kids can't get enough of the microwave. But will they still be fans when Paul shows them some of the things that may be lurking in their lunch? The team also looks at the bacteria that might be hiding inside some of the most popular convenience foods used by millions of families across the UK.(n)

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: On the outskirts of Chesterfield, Jim responds to an urgent call for back-up from his colleague Tom, who is surrounded by an angry mob following a report of a teenager driving dangerously. The aggressive young man, emboldened by an equally hostile crowd of friends, has no insurance and when police decide to seize and tow his car away, he takes out his frustrations on his own vehicle.(n)

programme.title: The Railway 24/7
programme.desc: Commercial director Darren launches a new scheme to tackle passengers not paying for tickets with on-the-spot fines and penalties. Meanwhile, passengers raise the alarm about an abandoned black rucksack on the Huddersfield to Manchester route, and in the maintenance depot, Carl and Steve are replacing a five-tonne bogie, the structure that connects the wheels to the carriages.(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Wars: Battle of the High Street
programme.desc: How retail giants have fought to win control of a multi-billion-pound industry, following the bitter battles that have taken place over the decades, starting in Victorian times as the likes of Tesco, Waitrose and Sainsbury's emerged. From the introduction of barcodes in the 1980s, to the ad wars that took place on TV screens in the 2000s, the programme reveals how the biggest brands have used every trick in the retail book to try to dominate the competition, but that none of the home-grown giants knew quite what to expect with the arrival of German competitors, Aldi and Lidl.(n)

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: The hospital security team is called to reception after a suspected drug user is found passed out on the floor, while ex-factory worker Claude is determined to discharge himself after a bang on the head. programme.desc: Cameras follow enforcement agencies carrying out raids across Britain, as they take drastic action against fraudsters, counterfeiters and criminals. Heading onwards to Vienna, the presenter is all set to meet a real-life princess. programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Seventeenth-century Dutch painter Jan Vermeer, the lowly son of an innkeeper and art dealer, lived an uneventful life, leaving fewer than 40 authenticated works and dying unknown outside his home town. Tim Marlow investigates how his talent came to be regarded as highly as Rembrandt's, and examines the simple domestic scenes that led to his posthumous rise to fame.(n)

programme.desc: High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Steve are sent to north London to evict a young Romanian mother who owes £6,000 in unpaid rent, but when they arrive, they discover a family home that is not quite what it appears to be. Meanwhile, in Kent, Brian and Alan turn up the pressure on a debtor who has admitted to owing more than £7,000, but claims he cannot afford to pay up - despite having a garage full of expensive tools. Later, in south-west London, Brian accompanies Delroy as he tries to recover a £400,000 debt from a residential property, and when the pair trace the defaulter to an upmarket cafe, the man in charge lies about his identity.(n)
